title: Lazy Sunday Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: A rich, slow-cooked Moroccan dish featuring lamb, maple syrup, and aromatics. Perfect for a lazy Sunday afternoon.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions and cook until they're softened and lightly browned, about 10 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for another minute.
Add the lamb shoulder and cook until it's browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the chicken broth and maple syrup, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then cover the pot and transfer it to the oven.
Braise the tagine in the preheated oven for 2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and falls apart easily.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Skim off any excess fat that rises to the surface, then serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Serve with crusty bread or over couscous, if desired.
